channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump’s Fund Puts Blanche in a Tight Spot as He Aims to Lead Justice Dept.
item.description: Once seen by some as the most conventional of President Trump’s political appointees, Todd Blanche has taken off the gloves in his new role as acting attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.category: House Select Committee to Investigate the January 6th Attack
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, during an appearance before the Senate appropriations subcommittee on Tuesday.

item.title: How the $1.8 Billion Trump Fund May Violate Past Practice and Policy
item.description: The fund that could offer payouts to Trump allies who accuse the government of mistreatment is not only highly unusual but also appears to violate the administration’s own policies.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:description: On Pam Bondi’s first day as attorney general in February 2025, she signed a directive that appeared to prohibit the kind of fund the Trump administration established this week.

item.title: Trump’s Government Moves to Spare an Unhappy Taxpayer Named Trump
item.description: No president has ever used the federal government to advance his own personal interests and those of his family and allies as expansively and openly as Mr. Trump has.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:description: President Trump seems even less inhibited by the rules, written or unwritten, that governed his predecessors.

item.title: Inside Trump’s Pressure Campaign on Cuba, and Why Food Banks Are Getting Squeezed
item.description: Plus, Stephen Colbert’s last show.
item.dc:creator: Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Michael Crowley and James Poniewozik
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole González for The New York Times
item.media:description: The indictment of Raúl Castro, the former president of Cuba, comes as the country’s oil supplies for domestic use and power plants have been exhausted.

item.title: Democrats’ Midterm Strength Masks Fierce Divides and Frustration, Poll Shows
item.description: Democratic voters are in a combative, anti-establishment mood, unhappy with their party and disagreeing about its best path forward, a New York Times/Siena poll found.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

item.title: ‘We’ve Never Been This Bad.’ Eastern Pennsylvania Weighs Rising Costs.
item.description: With two swing districts, the region from Scranton to Allentown could decide control of the House, and voters are expressing profound economic distress.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise and Hannah Beier
item.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Lehigh Valley in Pennsylvania includes midsize cities like Allentown that are growing increasingly diverse.

item.title: Trump Gets His Payback in Primary Elections, but It Comes at a Cost in Congress
item.description: Republican senators are angry the president is working to unseat their colleagues. But he is also creating more free agents in his own party in Congress willing to defy him.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:description: After losing his primary race to a Trump-backed challenger, Senator Bill Cassidy, Republican of Louisiana, voted with Democrats in support of a measure to limit the war in Iran.

item.title: Colorado Governor Censured for Commuting Sentence of Election Denier
item.description: The Colorado State Democratic Party, reflecting the anger of rank-and-file Democratic voters, rebuked Gov. Jared Polis on Wednesday over his decision to release Tina Peters from prison.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Michael Ciaglo for The New York Times
item.media:description: “The man is wildly out of touch with the party he effectively leads.” Gov. Jared Polis of Colorado, a Democrat, infuriated the party faithful when he commuted the prison sentence of an election denier.

item.title: News of U.S. Indictment Slow to Reach Cubans Waiting for a Breakthrough
item.description: While many Cubans were divided over the legitimacy of the U.S. charging Raul Castro with murder, the hope for developments that might ease their suffering is widespread. “This has to change.”
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole González for The New York Times
item.media:description: A furniture workshop in Havana carries reminders that the Castro family has been part of Cuba’s ruling elite for more than 65 years.

item.title: Justice Dept. Charges Raúl Castro as Trump Escalates Pressure Campaign Against Cuba
item.description: The indictment was an extraordinary escalation of the Trump administration’s multifaceted pressure campaign against Cuba’s Communist government.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer, Frances Robles and David C. Adams
item.media:description: The indictment of Raúl Castro, the former president of Cuba, comes as the country’s oil supplies for domestic use and power plants has been exhausted.

item.title: Nimitz Aircraft Carrier Enters Caribbean as Trump Pressures Cuba
item.description: The carrier arrived in the southern Caribbean on the same day that the Justice Department announced charges against Raúl Castro.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The aircraft carrier U.S.S. Nimitz in Guanabara Bay in Rio de Janeiro, earlier this month.

item.title: Experimental Drug Yields Dramatic Weight Loss
item.description: People who got the injection, retatrutide, lost 28 percent of their body weight on average after 80 weeks, Eli Lilly said.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata and Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: Eli Lilly’s headquarters in Indianapolis.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Final ‘Late Show’ Marks the End of an Era
item.description: Stephen Colbert’s “Colbert Report” satirized politics. Then his “Late Show” confronted a moment when politics became self-satirizing.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:credit: CQ Roll Call/Associated Press; Scott Kowalchyk/CBS

item.title: A Powerful El Niño Is Forming. If History Is a Guide, It Could Hit Hard.
item.description: The biggest episodes of the past have altered the course of human events, according to researchers. An emerging one is drawing historic comparisons.
item.dc:creator: Chico Harlan
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: An illustration published in 1877 depicted a scene in southern India. Millions died from a famine that coincided with an El Niño that year.

item.title: Trump Approved an Nvidia Chip for Sale in China. Beijing Doesn’t Want It.
item.description: The U.S. tech giant’s powerful H200 chip seemed poised to boost Beijing’s A.I. ambitions, but not a single one has been purchased in China.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: Jensen Huang, the chief executive of Nvidia, said he did not bring up the chip with Chinese officials in Beijing last week.

item.title: Bluesky Says Kremlin Is Hacking Its Platform to Spread Propaganda
item.description: The company said it was fighting Russian efforts to hijack real users’ accounts to post fake content, an apparently novel tactic.
item.dc:creator: Steven Lee Myers
item.media:credit: Davide Bonaldo/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Russia hacked into the accounts of real people on Bluesky to spread fake articles, the social media platform said.

item.title: Samsung Averts a Walkout With Big Bonuses, but Discord Over A.I. Profits Brews
item.description: Samsung Electronics, a global memory chip supplier, has been a major beneficiary of the A.I boom. It has also become the center of a debate over how to divide its spoils.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:description: Outside the Samsung Electronics semiconductor plant in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, on Thursday.

item.title: A $5 Million Donation From Big Tobacco Preceded F.D.A. Vape Decision
item.description: Reynolds American’s contribution to MAGA Inc. came about one week before the Trump administration announced a new policy on e-cigarettes the company had sought.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel and Christina Jewett
item.media:description: The F.D.A. has issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies including Reynolds American to begin selling flavored vapes.

item.title: The Hard-Line Military Fraternity Running Iran
item.description: Decision making in Iran is guided by a small group of men associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran showing the three supreme leaders, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomenei, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his son, Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: China’s Housing Slump Shows Signs of Bottoming Out. We’ve Been Here Before.
item.description: Property prices in Shanghai, in particular, are rebounding, but the national market still faces an enormous overhang — 90 million empty or unfinished apartments.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Many families in China poured their savings into apartments once seen as a safe path to wealth, only to watch values unravel.

item.title: How Mamdani’s Budget Announcement Went Viral Far Beyond New York
item.description: Mayor Mamdani’s executive budget was met with hero worship online. What did the social media chatter miss about the messy reality of municipal policymaking?
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s executive budget release earlier this month was given the star treatment by some of his followers on social media.

item.title: Mira Nair Finds a New Audience as Mother of Zohran Mamdani
item.description: “I feel like we have given him to the world,” the filmmaker said in a conversation with The New York Times. Her next movie is about one of India’s greatest artists.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das
item.media:credit: Atul Loke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mira Nair, the acclaimed film director and mother of Zohran Mamdani, in Amritsar, India, in March.

item.title: Thousands of N.Y.C. Jobs Could Be Lost to A.I. Boom, Report Says
item.description: A report by the New York City comptroller argues that the city’s economy will be transformed by artificial intelligence. “We’ve never seen anything like this,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Jesse Ilan Kornbluth for The New York Times
item.media:description: A report by Mark Levine, the New York City comptroller, marks the first major effort by the city’s leaders to engage with the looming question of how A.I. will affect it.

item.title: Bone Found Near Guthrie Home Predates Her Disappearance by 750 Years
item.description: Archaeological experts say the discovery is a cautionary tale about the consequences of citizen investigators taking matters into their own hands.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:description: Journalists gathered near the home of Nancy Guthrie after she went missing in Tucson, Ariz., in February.

item.title: Australian Swimmer Who Crashed Beach Fashion Show Becomes the Star
item.description: David Handley was going for a swim in Sydney when he found himself walking on an Australian Fashion Week runway. “I’ve usurped the spot of the lead model,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: You Can Give Knicks Fans T-Shirts. But Will They Wear Them? Well …
item.description: In arenas all across the league, N.B.A. fans embrace the monochrome T-shirt as a way to show team spirit. New Yorkers aren’t having it.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh

item.title: Trump Just Took Us Somewhere the Country Has Never Been Before
item.description: There’s no telling where this leads.
item.dc:creator: Noah Shachtman

item.title: This Is How a Party Ends Up Looking Like a Clown Car
item.description: We need leaders who tell us hard truths while insisting that the present state of our politics is not permanent.
item.dc:creator: Ben Rhodes

item.title: Intraparty Conflict
item.description: We look at the turmoil inside both political parties.
item.dc:creator: Adam B. Kushner
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters in Independence, Ky., on Tuesday.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Pays His Respects to ‘The Late Show with Stephen Colbert’
item.description: Kimmel said he hoped others will also tune in to CBS “for the last time” to watch Thursday’s “Late Show” finale.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: British Politics Is Obsessing Over the Bond Market. Here’s Why.
item.description: Britain is facing another round of turmoil in government leadership, jolting debt investors who were already on high alert over inflation.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Near the Bank of England.

item.title: 3 Die After Suspected Exposure to Unknown Substance in New Mexico
item.description: More than a dozen emergency officials were hospitalized after responding to a home in a town near Albuquerque. The authorities said there was no threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:description: Three people have died after being exposed to an unidentified substance in Mountainair, N.M.

item.title: Pigeons and People Have Been Frenemies for Longer Than You Think
item.description: Bones discovered at an archaeological site in Cyprus suggest the birds have been strutting around human settlements since at least 1400 B.C.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Nuwer
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new study leads scientists to wonder whether people set out to domesticate pigeons or the process occurred naturally as pigeons “colonized” human environments.

item.title: Was a Story That Just Won a Literary Prize A.I.-Generated?
item.description: A respected literary magazine has published an award-winning short story many readers believe to be generated by artificial intelligence. Experts aren’t all so sure.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
